prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
# Translation of "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."

## French
"Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n'importe quelle dispute aurait pu le faire."

*Note: The addition of "le faire" (to do it) at the end maintains the natural flow in French while preserving the comparative structure.*

## Japanese
"私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな口論よりも多くを語っていた。"
(Watashitachi no aida no chinmoku wa, donna kōron yori mo ōku wo katatte ita.)

*Note: The phrase "said more" is translated as "語っていた" (katatte ita) which literally means "was telling/narrating," as this conveys the metaphorical sense of silence communicating something.*

## Arabic
"كان الصمت بيننا يقول أكثر مما كانت ستقوله أي مجادلة."
(Kāna al-ṣamtu baynanā yaqūlu akthar mimmā kānat sataqūluhu ayyu mujādalah.)

*Note: The hypothetical aspect of "could have" required restructuring to "مما كانت ستقوله" (than what any argument would have said) to maintain the conditional meaning naturally in Arabic.*
